    X_train = train_df.drop(columns=['target'])
    y_train = train_df['target'].values

    test_df = pd.read_parquet(test_data_path)
    X_test = test_df.drop(columns=['target'])
    y_test = test_df['target'].values

    # Load config
    with open(preprocessing_config_path, 'r') as f:
        config = json.load(f)

    class_names = config['class_names']
    feature_columns = config['feature_columns']

    print(f"\n Training {model_name}...")
    print(f"   Class names: {class_names}")
    print(f"   Features: {len(feature_columns)}")

    # Preprocessing pipeline
    num_proc = Pipeline(steps=[
        ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="mean")),
        ("scaler", StandardScaler(with_mean=True, with_std=True))
    ])
    cat_proc = Pipeline(steps=[
        ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="most_frequent")),
        ("ohe", OneHotEncoder(handle_unknown="ignore", sparse_output=False))
    ])

    preprocess = ColumnTransformer(
        transformers=[
            ("num", num_proc, selector(dtype_include=np.number)),
            ("cat", cat_proc, selector(dtype_exclude=np.number))
        ],
        remainder="drop"
    )

    # Model selection
    model_map = {
        "KNN": KNeighborsClassifier(),
        "DecisionTree": DecisionTreeClassifier(class_weight="balanced", random_state=42),
        "RandomForest": RandomForestClassifier(class_weight="balanced", random_state=42, n_jobs=-1),
        "NaiveBayes": GaussianNB(),
        "LogisticRegression": LogisticRegression(class_weight="balanced", random_state=42, max_iter=1000),
        "SVM": SVC(class_weight="balanced", random_state=42)
    }

    clf = model_map[model_name]

    # Training pipeline
    training_pipeline = Pipeline([
        ("prep", preprocess), 
        ("clf", clf)
    ])

    with cf.start_run(run_name=f"{model_name}_training") as run:
        run_id = run.info.run_id

        # Log parameters
        cf.log_param("model_type", model_name)
        cf.log_param("n_samples_train", len(X_train))
        cf.log_param("n_samples_test", len(X_test))
        cf.log_param("class_names", ', '.join(class_names))
        cf.log_param("n_classes", len(class_names))

        # GridSearchCV
        cv = StratifiedKFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=42)
        gs = GridSearchCV(
            estimator=training_pipeline,
            param_grid=model_params,
            scoring="f1_macro",
            cv=cv,
            n_jobs=-1,
            refit=True,
            verbose=1
        )

        gs.fit(X_train, y_train)
        best_cv_score = gs.best_score_
        best_params = gs.best_params_

        print(f"-> {model_name}: CV F1 Macro = {best_cv_score:.4f}")
        print(f"   Best params: {best_params}")

        # Log best parameters
        for param_name, param_value in best_params.items():
            cf.log_param(param_name, param_value)

        cf.log_metric("cv_f1_macro", best_cv_score)
        cf.log_metric("cv_std", gs.cv_results_['std_test_score'][gs.best_index_])

        # Log CV results
        cv_results_df = pd.DataFrame(gs.cv_results_)
        cv_results_df.to_csv("cv_results.csv", index=False)
        cf.log_artifact("cv_results.csv")

        best_pipeline = gs.best_estimator_

        # Evaluate on test set
        print(f"\n Evaluating {model_name} on test set...")

        y_pred = best_pipeline.predict(X_test)

        test_f1_macro = f1_score(y_test, y_pred, average='macro')
        test_f1_weighted = f1_score(y_test, y_pred, average='weighted')
        test_accuracy = accuracy_score(y_test, y_pred)

        print(f" Test Performance:")
        print(f"   Accuracy: {test_accuracy:.4f}")
        print(f"   F1 Macro: {test_f1_macro:.4f}")
        print(f"   F1 Weighted: {test_f1_weighted:.4f}")

        cf.log_metric("test_f1_macro", test_f1_macro)
        cf.log_metric("test_f1_weighted", test_f1_weighted)
        cf.log_metric("test_accuracy", test_accuracy)

        # Log per-class metrics
        report = classification_report(y_test, y_pred, target_names=class_names, output_dict=True)
        for label, label_metrics in report.items():
            if isinstance(label_metrics, dict):
                for metric_name, value in label_metrics.items():
                    cf.log_metric(f"test_{label}_{metric_name}", value)

        # Log confusion matrix
        cm = confusion_matrix(y_test, y_pred)
        cm_dict = {"confusion_matrix": cm.tolist(), "labels": class_names}
        with open("confusion_matrix.json", "w") as f:
            json.dump(cm_dict, f, indent=2)
        cf.log_artifact("confusion_matrix.json")

        # Log confusion matrix visualization
        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 8))
        disp = ConfusionMatrixDisplay(confusion_matrix=cm, display_labels=class_names)
        disp.plot(ax=ax, xticks_rotation=45, colorbar=True, cmap='Blues')
        plt.title(f'Confusion Matrix - {model_name}')
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig('confusion_matrix.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
        cf.log_artifact('confusion_matrix.png')
        plt.close()

        # Create PyFunc model wrapper
        wrapped_model = LabelDecodingWrapper(
            sklearn_pipeline=best_pipeline,
            class_names=class_names,
            feature_columns=feature_columns
        )

        # Test with sample data
        sample_input = X_train.head(3)
        sample_pred = wrapped_model.predict(context=None, model_input=sample_input)
        print(f" Sample predictions: {sample_pred}")

        # Log model
        model_info = cf.pyfunc.log_model(
            artifact_path="model",
            python_model=wrapped_model,
            signature=cf.models.infer_signature(sample_input, sample_pred),
            input_example=sample_input
        )

        model_uri = model_info.model_uri

        print(f" Model logged to MLflow:")
        print(f"   Run ID: {run_id}")
        print(f"   Model URI: {model_uri}")

    outputs = namedtuple("Outputs", ["cv_f1_macro", "test_f1_macro", "test_accuracy", "run_id", "model_uri"])
    return outputs(float(best_cv_score), float(test_f1_macro), float(test_accuracy), run_id, model_uri)

def _serialize_float(float_value: float) -> str:
    if isinstance(float_value, str):
        return float_value
    if not isinstance(float_value, (float, int)):
        raise TypeError('Value "{}" has type "{}" instead of float.'.format(
            str(float_value), str(type(float_value))))
    return str(float_value)

def _serialize_str(str_value: str) -> str:
    if not isinstance(str_value, str):
        raise TypeError('Value "{}" has type "{}" instead of str.'.format(
            str(str_value), str(type(str_value))))
    return str_value

            df = model_input[self.feature_columns]

        gs.fit(X_train, y_train)
        best_cv_score = gs.best_score_
        best_params = gs.best_params_

        print(f"-> {model_name}: CV F1 Macro = {best_cv_score:.4f}")
        print(f"   Best params: {best_params}")

        # Log best parameters
        for param_name, param_value in best_params.items():
            cf.log_param(param_name, param_value)

        cf.log_metric("cv_f1_macro", best_cv_score)
        cf.log_metric("cv_std", gs.cv_results_['std_test_score'][gs.best_index_])

        # Log CV results
        cv_results_df = pd.DataFrame(gs.cv_results_)
        cv_results_df.to_csv("cv_results.csv", index=False)
        cf.log_artifact("cv_results.csv")

        best_pipeline = gs.best_estimator_

        # Evaluate on test set
        print(f"\n Evaluating {model_name} on test set...")

        y_pred = best_pipeline.predict(X_test)

        test_f1_macro = f1_score(y_test, y_pred, average='macro')
        test_f1_weighted = f1_score(y_test, y_pred, average='weighted')
        test_accuracy = accuracy_score(y_test, y_pred)

        print(f" Test Performance:")
        print(f"   Accuracy: {test_accuracy:.4f}")
        print(f"   F1 Macro: {test_f1_macro:.4f}")
        print(f"   F1 Weighted: {test_f1_weighted:.4f}")

        cf.log_metric("test_f1_macro", test_f1_macro)
        cf.log_metric("test_f1_weighted", test_f1_weighted)
        cf.log_metric("test_accuracy", test_accuracy)

        # Log per-class metrics
        report = classification_report(y_test, y_pred, target_names=class_names, output_dict=True)
        for label, label_metrics in report.items():
            if isinstance(label_metrics, dict):
                for metric_name, value in label_metrics.items():
                    cf.log_metric(f"test_{label}_{metric_name}", value)

        # Log confusion matrix
        cm = confusion_matrix(y_test, y_pred)
        cm_dict = {"confusion_matrix": cm.tolist(), "labels": class_names}
        with open("confusion_matrix.json", "w") as f:
            json.dump(cm_dict, f, indent=2)
        cf.log_artifact("confusion_matrix.json")

        # Log confusion matrix visualization
        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 8))
        disp = ConfusionMatrixDisplay(confusion_matrix=cm, display_labels=class_names)
        disp.plot(ax=ax, xticks_rotation=45, colorbar=True, cmap='Blues')
        plt.title(f'Confusion Matrix - {model_name}')
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig('confusion_matrix.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
        cf.log_artifact('confusion_matrix.png')
        plt.close()

        # Create PyFunc model wrapper
        wrapped_model = LabelDecodingWrapper(
            sklearn_pipeline=best_pipeline,
            class_names=class_names,
            feature_columns=feature_columns
        )

        # Test with sample data
        sample_input = X_train.head(3)
        sample_pred = wrapped_model.predict(context=None, model_input=sample_input)
        print(f" Sample predictions: {sample_pred}")

        # Log model
        model_info = cf.pyfunc.log_model(
            artifact_path="model",
            python_model=wrapped_model,
            signature=cf.models.infer_signature(sample_input, sample_pred),
            input_example=sample_input
        )

        model_uri = model_info.model_uri

        print(f" Model logged to MLflow:")
        print(f"   Run ID: {run_id}")
        print(f"   Model URI: {model_uri}")

    outputs = namedtuple("Outputs", ["cv_f1_macro", "test_f1_macro", "test_accuracy", "run_id", "model_uri"])
    return outputs(float(best_cv_score), float(test_f1_macro), float(test_accuracy), run_id, model_uri)

def _serialize_float(float_value: float) -> str:
    if isinstance(float_value, str):
        return float_value
    if not isinstance(float_value, (float, int)):
        raise TypeError('Value "{}" has type "{}" instead of float.'.format(

def _serialize_str(str_value: str) -> str:
    if not isinstance(str_value, str):

    return str_value

def train_and_evaluate_model(
    train_data_path,
    test_data_path,
    preprocessing_config_path,
    model_name,
    model_params,
    experiment_id
):
    """Train a model with GridSearchCV, evaluate it, and log everything to MLflow"""
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import json
    import mlflow as cf
    import mlflow.pyfunc
    from sklearn.model_selection import StratifiedKFold, GridSearchCV
    from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler, OneHotEncoder
    from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer, make_column_selector as selector
    from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
    from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
    from sklearn.neighbors import KNeighborsClassifier
    from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeClassifier
    from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
    from sklearn.naive_bayes import GaussianNB
    from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
    from sklearn.svm import SVC
    from sklearn.metrics import f1_score, classification_report, confusion_matrix, ConfusionMatrixDisplay, accuracy_score
    from collections import namedtuple
    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

    # MLflow PyFunc wrapper for KServe deployment with DataFrame support
    class LabelDecodingWrapper(cf.pyfunc.PythonModel):
        """
        MLflow PyFunc wrapper for KServe with dataframe_split format
        Receives DataFrame, returns class names
        """

        def __init__(self, sklearn_pipeline, class_names, feature_columns):
            self.sklearn_pipeline = sklearn_pipeline
            self.class_names = class_names
            self.feature_columns = feature_columns

        def predict(self, context, model_input):
            import pandas as pd
            import numpy as np

            if not isinstance(model_input, pd.DataFrame):
                raise ValueError(f"Expected DataFrame, got {type(model_input)}")

            # Validate and select features in correct order
            missing_features = set(self.feature_columns) - set(model_input.columns)
            if missing_features:
                raise ValueError(f"Missing features: {missing_features}")

            df = model_input[self.feature_columns]

            # Get numeric predictions and decode to class names
            numeric_preds = self.sklearn_pipeline.predict(df)
            return np.array([self.class_names[int(i)] for i in numeric_preds])

    cf.set_experiment(experiment_id=experiment_id)

    # Load data
    train_df = pd.read_parquet(train_data_path)
    X_train = train_df.drop(columns=['target'])
    y_train = train_df['target'].values

    test_df = pd.read_parquet(test_data_path)
    X_test = test_df.drop(columns=['target'])
    y_test = test_df['target'].values

    # Load config
    with open(preprocessing_config_path, 'r') as f:
        config = json.load(f)

    class_names = config['class_names']
    feature_columns = config['feature_columns']

    print(f"\n Training {model_name}...")
    print(f"   Class names: {class_names}")
    print(f"   Features: {len(feature_columns)}")

    # Preprocessing pipeline
    num_proc = Pipeline(steps=[
        ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="mean")),
        ("scaler", StandardScaler(with_mean=True, with_std=True))
    ])
    cat_proc = Pipeline(steps=[
        ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="most_frequent")),
        ("ohe", OneHotEncoder(handle_unknown="ignore", sparse_output=False))
    ])

    preprocess = ColumnTransformer(
        transformers=[
            ("num", num_proc, selector(dtype_include=np.number)),
            ("cat", cat_proc, selector(dtype_exclude=np.number))
        ],
        remainder="drop"
    )

    # Model selection
    model_map = {
        "KNN": KNeighborsClassifier(),
        "DecisionTree": DecisionTreeClassifier(class_weight="balanced", random_state=42),
        "RandomForest": RandomForestClassifier(class_weight="balanced", random_state=42, n_jobs=-1),
        "NaiveBayes": GaussianNB(),
        "LogisticRegression": LogisticRegression(class_weight="balanced", random_state=42, max_iter=1000),
        "SVM": SVC(class_weight="balanced", random_state=42)
    }

    clf = model_map[model_name]

    # Training pipeline
    training_pipeline = Pipeline([
        ("prep", preprocess), 
        ("clf", clf)
    ])

    with cf.start_run(run_name=f"{model_name}_training") as run:
        run_id = run.info.run_id

        # Log parameters
        cf.log_param("model_type", model_name)
        cf.log_param("n_samples_train", len(X_train))
        cf.log_param("n_samples_test", len(X_test))
        cf.log_param("class_names", ', '.join(class_names))
        cf.log_param("n_classes", len(class_names))

        # GridSearchCV
        cv = StratifiedKFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=42)
        gs = GridSearchCV(
            estimator=training_pipeline,
            param_grid=model_params,
            scoring="f1_macro",
            cv=cv,
            n_jobs=-1,
            refit=True,
            verbose=1
        )

        gs.fit(X_train, y_train)
        best_cv_score = gs.best_score_
        best_params = gs.best_params_

        print(f"-> {model_name}: CV F1 Macro = {best_cv_score:.4f}")
        print(f"   Best params: {best_params}")

        # Log best parameters
        for param_name, param_value in best_params.items():
            cf.log_param(param_name, param_value)

        cf.log_metric("cv_f1_macro", best_cv_score)
        cf.log_metric("cv_std", gs.cv_results_['std_test_score'][gs.best_index_])

        # Log CV results
        cv_results_df = pd.DataFrame(gs.cv_results_)
        cv_results_df.to_csv("cv_results.csv", index=False)
        cf.log_artifact("cv_results.csv")

        best_pipeline = gs.best_estimator_

        # Evaluate on test set
        print(f"\n Evaluating {model_name} on test set...")

        y_pred = best_pipeline.predict(X_test)

        test_f1_macro = f1_score(y_test, y_pred, average='macro')
        test_f1_weighted = f1_score(y_test, y_pred, average='weighted')
        test_accuracy = accuracy_score(y_test, y_pred)

        print(f" Test Performance:")
        print(f"   Accuracy: {test_accuracy:.4f}")
        print(f"   F1 Macro: {test_f1_macro:.4f}")
        print(f"   F1 Weighted: {test_f1_weighted:.4f}")

        cf.log_metric("test_f1_macro", test_f1_macro)
        cf.log_metric("test_f1_weighted", test_f1_weighted)
        cf.log_metric("test_accuracy", test_accuracy)

        # Log per-class metrics
        report = classification_report(y_test, y_pred, target_names=class_names, output_dict=True)
        for label, label_metrics in report.items():
            if isinstance(label_metrics, dict):
                for metric_name, value in label_metrics.items():
                    cf.log_metric(f"test_{label}_{metric_name}", value)

        # Log confusion matrix
        cm = confusion_matrix(y_test, y_pred)
        cm_dict = {"confusion_matrix": cm.tolist(), "labels": class_names}
        with open("confusion_matrix.json", "w") as f:
            json.dump(cm_dict, f, indent=2)
        cf.log_artifact("confusion_matrix.json")

        # Log confusion matrix visualization
        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 8))
        disp = ConfusionMatrixDisplay(confusion_matrix=cm, display_labels=class_names)
        disp.plot(ax=ax, xticks_rotation=45, colorbar=True, cmap='Blues')
        plt.title(f'Confusion Matrix - {model_name}')
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig('confusion_matrix.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
        cf.log_artifact('confusion_matrix.png')
        plt.close()

        # Create PyFunc model wrapper
        wrapped_model = LabelDecodingWrapper(
            sklearn_pipeline=best_pipeline,
            class_names=class_names,
            feature_columns=feature_columns
        )

        # Test with sample data
        sample_input = X_train.head(3)
        sample_pred = wrapped_model.predict(context=None, model_input=sample_input)
        print(f" Sample predictions: {sample_pred}")

        # Log model
        model_info = cf.pyfunc.log_model(
            artifact_path="model",
            python_model=wrapped_model,
            signature=cf.models.infer_signature(sample_input, sample_pred),
            input_example=sample_input
        )

        model_uri = model_info.model_uri

        print(f" Model logged to MLflow:")
        print(f"   Run ID: {run_id}")
        print(f"   Model URI: {model_uri}")

    outputs = namedtuple("Outputs", ["cv_f1_macro", "test_f1_macro", "test_accuracy", "run_id", "model_uri"])
    return outputs(float(best_cv_score), float(test_f1_macro), float(test_accuracy), run_id, model_uri)

def _serialize_float(float_value: float) -> str:
    if isinstance(float_value, str):
        return float_value
    if not isinstance(float_value, (float, int)):
        raise TypeError('Value "{}" has type "{}" instead of float.'.format(
            str(float_value), str(type(float_value))))
    return str(float_value)

def _serialize_str(str_value: str) -> str:
    if not isinstance(str_value, str):
        raise TypeError('Value "{}" has type "{}" instead of str.'.format(
            str(str_value), str(type(str_value))))
    return str_value

        # Create PyFunc model wrapper

        )

        print(f"   Run ID: {run_id}")
        print(f"   Model URI: {model_uri}")

    outputs = namedtuple("Outputs", ["cv_f1_macro", "test_f1_macro", "test_accuracy", "run_id", "model_uri"])

pipelines.kubeflow.org/component_spec.name: Train and evaluate model

command: (PIP_DISABLE_PIP_VERSION_CHECK=1 python3 -m pip install --quiet --no-warn-script-location 'matplotlib==3.7.2' || PIP_DISABLE_PIP_VERSION_CHECK=1 python3 -m pip install --quiet --no-warn-script-location 'matplotlib==3.7.2' --user) && "$0" "$@"

def train_and_evaluate_model(
    train_data_path,
    test_data_path,
    preprocessing_config_path,
    model_name,
    model_params,
    experiment_id
):
    """Train a model with GridSearchCV, evaluate it, and log everything to MLflow"""
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import json
    import mlflow as cf
    import mlflow.pyfunc
    from sklearn.model_selection import StratifiedKFold, GridSearchCV
    from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler, OneHotEncoder
    from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer, make_column_selector as selector
    from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
    from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
    from sklearn.neighbors import KNeighborsClassifier
    from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeClassifier
    from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
    from sklearn.naive_bayes import GaussianNB
    from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
    from sklearn.svm import SVC
    from sklearn.metrics import f1_score, classification_report, confusion_matrix, ConfusionMatrixDisplay, accuracy_score
    from collections import namedtuple
    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

    # MLflow PyFunc wrapper for KServe deployment with DataFrame support
    class LabelDecodingWrapper(cf.pyfunc.PythonModel):
        """
        MLflow PyFunc wrapper for KServe with dataframe_split format
        Receives DataFrame, returns class names
        """

        def __init__(self, sklearn_pipeline, class_names, feature_columns):
            self.sklearn_pipeline = sklearn_pipeline
            self.class_names = class_names
            self.feature_columns = feature_columns

        def predict(self, context, model_input):
            import pandas as pd
            import numpy as np

            if not isinstance(model_input, pd.DataFrame):
                raise ValueError(f"Expected DataFrame, got {type(model_input)}")

            # Validate and select features in correct order
            missing_features = set(self.feature_columns) - set(model_input.columns)
            if missing_features:
                raise ValueError(f"Missing features: {missing_features}")

            df = model_input[self.feature_columns]

            # Get numeric predictions and decode to class names
            numeric_preds = self.sklearn_pipeline.predict(df)
            return np.array([self.class_names[int(i)] for i in numeric_preds])

    cf.set_experiment(experiment_id=experiment_id)

    # Load data
    train_df = pd.read_parquet(train_data_path)
    X_train = train_df.drop(columns=['target'])
    y_train = train_df['target'].values

    test_df = pd.read_parquet(test_data_path)
    X_test = test_df.drop(columns=['target'])
    y_test = test_df['target'].values

    # Load config
    with open(preprocessing_config_path, 'r') as f:
        config = json.load(f)

    class_names = config['class_names']
    feature_columns = config['feature_columns']

    print(f"\n Training {model_name}...")
    print(f"   Class names: {class_names}")
    print(f"   Features: {len(feature_columns)}")

    # Preprocessing pipeline
    num_proc = Pipeline(steps=[
        ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="mean")),
        ("scaler", StandardScaler(with_mean=True, with_std=True))
    ])
    cat_proc = Pipeline(steps=[
        ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="most_frequent")),
        ("ohe", OneHotEncoder(handle_unknown="ignore", sparse_output=False))
    ])

    preprocess = ColumnTransformer(
        transformers=[
            ("num", num_proc, selector(dtype_include=np.number)),
            ("cat", cat_proc, selector(dtype_exclude=np.number))
        ],
        remainder="drop"
    )

    # Model selection
    model_map = {
        "KNN": KNeighborsClassifier(),
        "DecisionTree": DecisionTreeClassifier(class_weight="balanced", random_state=42),
        "RandomForest": RandomForestClassifier(class_weight="balanced", random_state=42, n_jobs=-1),
        "NaiveBayes": GaussianNB(),
        "LogisticRegression": LogisticRegression(class_weight="balanced", random_state=42, max_iter=1000),
        "SVM": SVC(class_weight="balanced", random_state=42)
    }

    clf = model_map[model_name]

    # Training pipeline
    training_pipeline = Pipeline([
        ("prep", preprocess), 
        ("clf", clf)
    ])

    with cf.start_run(run_name=f"{model_name}_training") as run:
        run_id = run.info.run_id

        # Log parameters
        cf.log_param("model_type", model_name)
        cf.log_param("n_samples_train", len(X_train))
        cf.log_param("n_samples_test", len(X_test))
        cf.log_param("class_names", ', '.join(class_names))
        cf.log_param("n_classes", len(class_names))

        # GridSearchCV
        cv = StratifiedKFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=42)
        gs = GridSearchCV(
            estimator=training_pipeline,
            param_grid=model_params,
            scoring="f1_macro",
            cv=cv,
            n_jobs=-1,
            refit=True,
            verbose=1
        )

        gs.fit(X_train, y_train)
        best_cv_score = gs.best_score_
        best_params = gs.best_params_

        print(f"-> {model_name}: CV F1 Macro = {best_cv_score:.4f}")
        print(f"   Best params: {best_params}")

        # Log best parameters
        for param_name, param_value in best_params.items():
            cf.log_param(param_name, param_value)

        cf.log_metric("cv_f1_macro", best_cv_score)
        cf.log_metric("cv_std", gs.cv_results_['std_test_score'][gs.best_index_])

        # Log CV results
        cv_results_df = pd.DataFrame(gs.cv_results_)
        cv_results_df.to_csv("cv_results.csv", index=False)
        cf.log_artifact("cv_results.csv")

        best_pipeline = gs.best_estimator_

        # Evaluate on test set
        print(f"\n Evaluating {model_name} on test set...")

        y_pred = best_pipeline.predict(X_test)

        test_f1_macro = f1_score(y_test, y_pred, average='macro')
        test_f1_weighted = f1_score(y_test, y_pred, average='weighted')
        test_accuracy = accuracy_score(y_test, y_pred)

        print(f" Test Performance:")
        print(f"   Accuracy: {test_accuracy:.4f}")
        print(f"   F1 Macro: {test_f1_macro:.4f}")
        print(f"   F1 Weighted: {test_f1_weighted:.4f}")

        cf.log_metric("test_f1_macro", test_f1_macro)
        cf.log_metric("test_f1_weighted", test_f1_weighted)
        cf.log_metric("test_accuracy", test_accuracy)

        # Log per-class metrics
        report = classification_report(y_test, y_pred, target_names=class_names, output_dict=True)
        for label, label_metrics in report.items():
            if isinstance(label_metrics, dict):
                for metric_name, value in label_metrics.items():
                    cf.log_metric(f"test_{label}_{metric_name}", value)

        # Log confusion matrix
        cm = confusion_matrix(y_test, y_pred)
        cm_dict = {"confusion_matrix": cm.tolist(), "labels": class_names}
        with open("confusion_matrix.json", "w") as f:
            json.dump(cm_dict, f, indent=2)
        cf.log_artifact("confusion_matrix.json")

        # Log confusion matrix visualization
        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 8))
        disp = ConfusionMatrixDisplay(confusion_matrix=cm, display_labels=class_names)
        disp.plot(ax=ax, xticks_rotation=45, colorbar=True, cmap='Blues')
        plt.title(f'Confusion Matrix - {model_name}')
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig('confusion_matrix.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
        cf.log_artifact('confusion_matrix.png')
        plt.close()

        # Create PyFunc model wrapper
        wrapped_model = LabelDecodingWrapper(
            sklearn_pipeline=best_pipeline,
            class_names=class_names,
            feature_columns=feature_columns
        )

        # Test with sample data
        sample_input = X_train.head(3)
        sample_pred = wrapped_model.predict(context=None, model_input=sample_input)
        print(f" Sample predictions: {sample_pred}")

        # Log model
        model_info = cf.pyfunc.log_model(
            artifact_path="model",
            python_model=wrapped_model,
            signature=cf.models.infer_signature(sample_input, sample_pred),
            input_example=sample_input
        )

        model_uri = model_info.model_uri

        print(f" Model logged to MLflow:")
        print(f"   Run ID: {run_id}")
        print(f"   Model URI: {model_uri}")

    outputs = namedtuple("Outputs", ["cv_f1_macro", "test_f1_macro", "test_accuracy", "run_id", "model_uri"])
    return outputs(float(best_cv_score), float(test_f1_macro), float(test_accuracy), run_id, model_uri)

def _serialize_float(float_value: float) -> str:
    if isinstance(float_value, str):
        return float_value
    if not isinstance(float_value, (float, int)):
        raise TypeError('Value "{}" has type "{}" instead of float.'.format(
            str(float_value), str(type(float_value))))
    return str(float_value)

def _serialize_str(str_value: str) -> str:
    if not isinstance(str_value, str):
        raise TypeError('Value "{}" has type "{}" instead of str.'.format(
            str(str_value), str(type(str_value))))
    return str_value

pipelines.kubeflow.org/component_spec.name: Train and evaluate model

command: (PIP_DISABLE_PIP_VERSION_CHECK=1 python3 -m pip install --quiet --no-warn-script-location 'matplotlib==3.7.2' || PIP_DISABLE_PIP_VERSION_CHECK=1 python3 -m pip install --quiet --no-warn-script-location 'matplotlib==3.7.2' --user) && "$0" "$@"

def train_and_evaluate_model(
    train_data_path,
    test_data_path,
    preprocessing_config_path,
    model_name,
    model_params,
    experiment_id
):
    """Train a model with GridSearchCV, evaluate it, and log everything to MLflow"""
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import json
    import mlflow as cf
    import mlflow.pyfunc
    from sklearn.model_selection import StratifiedKFold, GridSearchCV
    from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler, OneHotEncoder
    from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer, make_column_selector as selector
    from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
    from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
    from sklearn.neighbors import KNeighborsClassifier
    from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeClassifier
    from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
    from sklearn.naive_bayes import GaussianNB
    from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
    from sklearn.svm import SVC
    from sklearn.metrics import f1_score, classification_report, confusion_matrix, ConfusionMatrixDisplay, accuracy_score
    from collections import namedtuple
    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

    # MLflow PyFunc wrapper for KServe deployment with DataFrame support
    class LabelDecodingWrapper(cf.pyfunc.PythonModel):
        """
        MLflow PyFunc wrapper for KServe with dataframe_split format
        Receives DataFrame, returns class names
        """

        def __init__(self, sklearn_pipeline, class_names, feature_columns):
            self.sklearn_pipeline = sklearn_pipeline
            self.class_names = class_names
            self.feature_columns = feature_columns

        def predict(self, context, model_input):
            import pandas as pd
            import numpy as np

            if not isinstance(model_input, pd.DataFrame):
                raise ValueError(f"Expected DataFrame, got {type(model_input)}")

            # Validate and select features in correct order
            missing_features = set(self.feature_columns) - set(model_input.columns)
            if missing_features:
                raise ValueError(f"Missing features: {missing_features}")

            df = model_input[self.feature_columns]

            # Get numeric predictions and decode to class names
            numeric_preds = self.sklearn_pipeline.predict(df)
            return np.array([self.class_names[int(i)] for i in numeric_preds])

    cf.set_experiment(experiment_id=experiment_id)

    # Load data
    train_df = pd.read_parquet(train_data_path)
    X_train = train_df.drop(columns=['target'])
    y_train = train_df['target'].values

    test_df = pd.read_parquet(test_data_path)
    X_test = test_df.drop(columns=['target'])
    y_test = test_df['target'].values

    # Load config
    with open(preprocessing_config_path, 'r') as f:
        config = json.load(f)

    class_names = config['class_names']
    feature_columns = config['feature_columns']

    print(f"\n Training {model_name}...")
    print(f"   Class names: {class_names}")
    print(f"   Features: {len(feature_columns)}")

    # Preprocessing pipeline
    num_proc = Pipeline(steps=[
        ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="mean")),
        ("scaler", StandardScaler(with_mean=True, with_std=True))
    ])
    cat_proc = Pipeline(steps=[
        ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="most_frequent")),
        ("ohe", OneHotEncoder(handle_unknown="ignore", sparse_output=False))
    ])

    preprocess = ColumnTransformer(
        transformers=[
            ("num", num_proc, selector(dtype_include=np.number)),
            ("cat", cat_proc, selector(dtype_exclude=np.number))
        ],
        remainder="drop"
    )

    # Model selection
    model_map = {
        "KNN": KNeighborsClassifier(),
        "DecisionTree": DecisionTreeClassifier(class_weight="balanced", random_state=42),
        "RandomForest": RandomForestClassifier(class_weight="balanced", random_state=42, n_jobs=-1),
        "NaiveBayes": GaussianNB(),
        "LogisticRegression": LogisticRegression(class_weight="balanced", random_state=42, max_iter=1000),
        "SVM": SVC(class_weight="balanced", random_state=42)
    }

    clf = model_map[model_name]

    # Training pipeline
    training_pipeline = Pipeline([
        ("prep", preprocess), 
        ("clf", clf)
    ])

    with cf.start_run(run_name=f"{model_name}_training") as run:
        run_id = run.info.run_id

        # Log parameters
        cf.log_param("model_type", model_name)
        cf.log_param("n_samples_train", len(X_train))
        cf.log_param("n_samples_test", len(X_test))
        cf.log_param("class_names", ', '.join(class_names))
        cf.log_param("n_classes", len(class_names))

        # GridSearchCV
        cv = StratifiedKFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=42)
        gs = GridSearchCV(
            estimator=training_pipeline,
            param_grid=model_params,
            scoring="f1_macro",
            cv=cv,
            n_jobs=-1,
            refit=True,
            verbose=1
        )

        gs.fit(X_train, y_train)
        best_cv_score = gs.best_score_
        best_params = gs.best_params_

        print(f"-> {model_name}: CV F1 Macro = {best_cv_score:.4f}")
        print(f"   Best params: {best_params}")

        # Log best parameters
        for param_name, param_value in best_params.items():
            cf.log_param(param_name, param_value)

        cf.log_metric("cv_f1_macro", best_cv_score)
        cf.log_metric("cv_std", gs.cv_results_['std_test_score'][gs.best_index_])

        # Log CV results
        cv_results_df = pd.DataFrame(gs.cv_results_)
        cv_results_df.to_csv("cv_results.csv", index=False)
        cf.log_artifact("cv_results.csv")

        best_pipeline = gs.best_estimator_

        # Evaluate on test set
        print(f"\n Evaluating {model_name} on test set...")

        y_pred = best_pipeline.predict(X_test)

        test_f1_macro = f1_score(y_test, y_pred, average='macro')
        test_f1_weighted = f1_score(y_test, y_pred, average='weighted')
        test_accuracy = accuracy_score(y_test, y_pred)

        print(f" Test Performance:")
        print(f"   Accuracy: {test_accuracy:.4f}")
        print(f"   F1 Macro: {test_f1_macro:.4f}")
        print(f"   F1 Weighted: {test_f1_weighted:.4f}")

        cf.log_metric("test_f1_macro", test_f1_macro)
        cf.log_metric("test_f1_weighted", test_f1_weighted)
        cf.log_metric("test_accuracy", test_accuracy)

        # Log per-class metrics
        report = classification_report(y_test, y_pred, target_names=class_names, output_dict=True)
        for label, label_metrics in report.items():
            if isinstance(label_metrics, dict):
                for metric_name, value in label_metrics.items():
                    cf.log_metric(f"test_{label}_{metric_name}", value)

        # Log confusion matrix
        cm = confusion_matrix(y_test, y_pred)
        cm_dict = {"confusion_matrix": cm.tolist(), "labels": class_names}
        with open("confusion_matrix.json", "w") as f:
            json.dump(cm_dict, f, indent=2)
        cf.log_artifact("confusion_matrix.json")

        # Log confusion matrix visualization
        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 8))
        disp = ConfusionMatrixDisplay(confusion_matrix=cm, display_labels=class_names)
        disp.plot(ax=ax, xticks_rotation=45, colorbar=True, cmap='Blues')
        plt.title(f'Confusion Matrix - {model_name}')
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig('confusion_matrix.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
        cf.log_artifact('confusion_matrix.png')
        plt.close()

        # Create PyFunc model wrapper
        wrapped_model = LabelDecodingWrapper(
            sklearn_pipeline=best_pipeline,
            class_names=class_names,
            feature_columns=feature_columns
        )

        # Test with sample data
        sample_input = X_train.head(3)
        sample_pred = wrapped_model.predict(context=None, model_input=sample_input)
        print(f" Sample predictions: {sample_pred}")

        # Log model
        model_info = cf.pyfunc.log_model(
            artifact_path="model",
            python_model=wrapped_model,
            signature=cf.models.infer_signature(sample_input, sample_pred),
            input_example=sample_input
        )

        model_uri = model_info.model_uri

        print(f" Model logged to MLflow:")
        print(f"   Run ID: {run_id}")
        print(f"   Model URI: {model_uri}")

    outputs = namedtuple("Outputs", ["cv_f1_macro", "test_f1_macro", "test_accuracy", "run_id", "model_uri"])
    return outputs(float(best_cv_score), float(test_f1_macro), float(test_accuracy), run_id, model_uri)

def _serialize_float(float_value: float) -> str:
    if isinstance(float_value, str):
        return float_value
    if not isinstance(float_value, (float, int)):
        raise TypeError('Value "{}" has type "{}" instead of float.'.format(
            str(float_value), str(type(float_value))))
    return str(float_value)

def _serialize_str(str_value: str) -> str:
    if not isinstance(str_value, str):
        raise TypeError('Value "{}" has type "{}" instead of str.'.format(
            str(str_value), str(type(str_value))))
    return str_value

def update_serve_model(model_id, isvc_name,preprocessing_config_path):
    import cogflow as cf
    import json
    ###  "feature_columns": [
    #"instance","pod",
    #"container_cpu_usage_seconds_total",
    #"container_cpu_system_seconds_total",
    #"container_memory_working_set_bytes",
    #"container_memory_rss",
    #"container_network_receive_bytes_total",

    all_labels={'instance','job','pod','node'}

    transformer_parameters={
        'PROMETHEUS_URL':'http://51.44.28.47:30045',
        'PROMETHEUS_METRICS':'',
        'EXTRACT_LABELS': '',
        'INCLUDE_INPUT_DATA': True,
        'MAX_WORKERS':20

    }
    # 0.19.15 works
    transformer_image="hiroregistry/k8-transformer:v0.20.0"
    with open(preprocessing_config_path, 'r') as f:
        config = json.load(f)
        features=config['feature_columns']
        metrics= [item for item in features if item not in all_labels]
        labels= [item for item in features if item in all_labels]
        metrics_str=','.join(metrics)
        labels_str=','.join(labels)
        transformer_parameters['PROMETHEUS_METRICS'] = metrics_str
        transformer_parameters['EXTRACT_LABELS'] = labels_str

        lists = cf.get_served_models(isvc_name=isvc_name)
        if len(lists)>0:
            cf.update_served_model(
                model_id=model_id, 
                isvc_name=isvc_name,
                transformer_image=transformer_image,
                transformer_parameters=transformer_parameters
            )       
        else:
            cf.serve_model(
                model_id=model_id, 
                isvc_name=isvc_name,
                transformer_image=transformer_image,
                transformer_parameters=transformer_parameters,protocol_version='v2'
                      )

    modelurl=cf.get_model_url(isvc_name=isvc_name)
    return json.dumps(modelurl)

def _make_parent_dirs_and_return_path(file_path: str):
    import os
    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(file_path), exist_ok=True)
    return file_path

def prepare_data(
    dataset_id,
    train_data_path,
    test_data_path,
    preprocessing_config_path,
    experiment_name,

):
    """Prepare train/test split and save preprocessing artifacts"""
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import cogflow as cf
    import json
    from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
    from sklearn.preprocessing import LabelEncoder
    from collections import namedtuple

    cf.download_dataset(dataset_id,"dataset.csv")
    df = pd.read_csv("dataset.csv")

    print(" Class Distribution:")
    print(df["Abnormality class"].value_counts())

    X = df.drop(columns=[
        "timestamp", "Abnormality class", "Microservice", 
        "Experiment", "BaseMicroservice", "InjectionStart", "InjectionEnd"
    ], errors="ignore")
    y = df["Abnormality class"].astype(str)

    le = LabelEncoder()
    y_enc = le.fit_transform(y)
    class_names = list(le.classes_)

    print("\n Class label mapping:")
    for idx, class_name in enumerate(class_names):
        print(f"  {idx} -> {class_name}")

    X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(
        X, y_enc, test_size=0.2, stratify=y_enc, random_state=42
    )

    train_df = pd.concat([X_train.reset_index(drop=True), 
                          pd.Series(y_train, name='target')], axis=1)
    test_df = pd.concat([X_test.reset_index(drop=True), 
                         pd.Series(y_test, name='target')], axis=1)

    train_df.to_parquet(train_data_path, index=False)
    test_df.to_parquet(test_data_path, index=False)

    prep_config = {
        "feature_columns": X.columns.tolist(),
        "class_names": class_names,
        "n_classes": len(class_names)
    }
    with open(preprocessing_config_path, 'w') as f:
        json.dump(prep_config, f, indent=2)

    print(f"Train shape: {X_train.shape} | Test shape: {X_test.shape}")

    import cogflow as cf
    experiment = cf.set_experiment(experiment_name)

    outputs = namedtuple("Outputs", ["class_names", "n_classes", "experiment_id"])

    return outputs(class_names, len(class_names), experiment.experiment_id)

def _serialize_int(int_value: int) -> str:
    if isinstance(int_value, str):
        return int_value
    if not isinstance(int_value, int):
        raise TypeError('Value "{}" has type "{}" instead of int.'.format(
            str(int_value), str(type(int_value))))
    return str(int_value)

def _serialize_json(obj) -> str:
    if isinstance(obj, str):
        return obj
    import json

    def default_serializer(obj):
        if hasattr(obj, 'to_struct'):
            return obj.to_struct()
        else:
            raise TypeError(
                "Object of type '%s' is not JSON serializable and does not have .to_struct() method."
                % obj.__class__.__name__)

    return json.dumps(obj, default=default_serializer, sort_keys=True)

def _serialize_str(str_value: str) -> str:
    if not isinstance(str_value, str):
        raise TypeError('Value "{}" has type "{}" instead of str.'.format(
            str(str_value), str(type(str_value))))
    return str_value

import argparse
_parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(prog='Prepare data', description='Prepare train/test split and save preprocessing artifacts')
_parser.add_argument("--dataset-id", dest="dataset_id", type=int, required=True, default=argparse.SUPPRESS)
_parser.add_argument("--experiment-name", dest="experiment_name", type=str, required=True, default=argparse.SUPPRESS)
_parser.add_argument("--train-data", dest="train_data_path", type=_make_parent_dirs_and_return_path, required=True, default=argparse.SUPPRESS)
_parser.add_argument("--test-data", dest="test_data_path", type=_make_parent_dirs_and_return_path, required=True, default=argparse.SUPPRESS)
_parser.add_argument("--preprocessing-config", dest="preprocessing_config_path", type=_make_parent_dirs_and_return_path, required=True, default=argparse.SUPPRESS)
_parser.add_argument("----output-paths", dest="_output_paths", type=str, nargs=3)
_parsed_args = vars(_parser.parse_args())
_output_files = _parsed_args.pop("_output_paths", [])

_outputs = prepare_data(**_parsed_args)

_output_serializers = [
    _serialize_json,
    _serialize_int,
    _serialize_str,

]

import os
for idx, output_file in enumerate(_output_files):
    try:
        os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(output_file))
    except OSError:
        pass
    with open(output_file, 'w') as f:
        f.write(_output_serializers[idx](_outputs[idx]))

    )

    train_df = pd.concat([X_train.reset_index(drop=True), 
                          pd.Series(y_train, name='target')], axis=1)
    test_df = pd.concat([X_test.reset_index(drop=True), 
                         pd.Series(y_test, name='target')], axis=1)

    train_df.to_parquet(train_data_path, index=False)
    test_df.to_parquet(test_data_path, index=False)

    prep_config = {
        "feature_columns": X.columns.tolist(),
        "class_names": class_names,
        "n_classes": len(class_names)
    }
    with open(preprocessing_config_path, 'w') as f:
        json.dump(prep_config, f, indent=2)

    print(f"Train shape: {X_train.shape} | Test shape: {X_test.shape}")

    import cogflow as cf
    experiment = cf.set_experiment(experiment_name)

    outputs = namedtuple("Outputs", ["class_names", "n_classes", "experiment_id"])

    return outputs(class_names, len(class_names), experiment.experiment_id)

def _serialize_int(int_value: int) -> str:
    if isinstance(int_value, str):
        return int_value
    if not isinstance(int_value, int):
        raise TypeError('Value "{}" has type "{}" instead of int.'.format(
            str(int_value), str(type(int_value))))
    return str(int_value)

def _serialize_json(obj) -> str:
    if isinstance(obj, str):
        return obj
    import json

    def default_serializer(obj):
        if hasattr(obj, 'to_struct'):
            return obj.to_struct()
        else:
            raise TypeError(
                "Object of type '%s' is not JSON serializable and does not have .to_struct() method."
                % obj.__class__.__name__)

    return json.dumps(obj, default=default_serializer, sort_keys=True)

def _serialize_str(str_value: str) -> str:
    if not isinstance(str_value, str):
        raise TypeError('Value "{}" has type "{}" instead of str.'.format(
            str(str_value), str(type(str_value))))
    return str_value

import argparse
_parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(prog='Prepare data', description='Prepare train/test split and save preprocessing artifacts')
_parser.add_argument("--dataset-id", dest="dataset_id", type=int, required=True, default=argparse.SUPPRESS)
_parser.add_argument("--experiment-name", dest="experiment_name", type=str, required=True, default=argparse.SUPPRESS)
_parser.add_argument("--train-data", dest="train_data_path", type=_make_parent_dirs_and_return_path, required=True, default=argparse.SUPPRESS)
_parser.add_argument("--test-data", dest="test_data_path", type=_make_parent_dirs_and_return_path, required=True, default=argparse.SUPPRESS)

_parser.add_argument("----output-paths", dest="_output_paths", type=str, nargs=3)
_parsed_args = vars(_parser.parse_args())
_output_files = _parsed_args.pop("_output_paths", [])

_outputs = prepare_data(**_parsed_args)

_output_serializers = [
    _serialize_json,
    _serialize_int,
    _serialize_str,

]

import os
for idx, output_file in enumerate(_output_files):
    try:
        os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(output_file))
    except OSError:
        pass
    with open(output_file, 'w') as f:
        f.write(_output_serializers[idx](_outputs[idx]))

def select_best_model(
    experiment_id,
    metricname = "cv_f1_macro"
):
    """Select the best model based on CV F1 macro score and register it to MLflow Model Registry"""
    from collections import namedtuple
    import cogflow as cf
    import json

    client = cf.mlflow.tracking.client.MlflowClient()
    runs = client.search_runs(
        experiment_ids=[experiment_id],
        order_by=[f"metrics.{metricname} DESC"],
        max_results=10
    )

    # Check if list is empty
    if not runs or len(runs) == 0:
        raise ValueError("No runs found in experiment")

    # Get the first run (best run - already sorted by metric DESC)
    best_run = runs[0]
    best_run_id = best_run.info.run_id

    # Access parameters and metrics correctly
    best_model_name = best_run.data.params.get('model_type', 'Unknown')
    best_cv = best_run.data.metrics.get('cv_f1_macro', 0.0)
    best_test = best_run.data.metrics.get('test_f1_macro', 0.0)
    best_test_acc = best_run.data.metrics.get('test_accuracy', 0.0)

    print(f" Best Model: {best_model_name}")
    print(f"   CV F1 Macro: {best_cv:.4f}")
    print(f"   Test F1 Macro: {best_test:.4f}")
    print(f"   Test Accuracy: {best_test_acc:.4f}")
    print(f"   Run ID: {best_run_id}")

    # Get model URI
    best_model_uri = f"runs:/{best_run_id}/model"

    return best_run_id

def train_and_evaluate_model(
    train_data_path,
    test_data_path,
    preprocessing_config_path,
    model_name,
    model_params,
    experiment_id
):
    """Train a model with GridSearchCV, evaluate it, and log everything to MLflow"""
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import json
    import mlflow as cf
    import mlflow.pyfunc
    from sklearn.model_selection import StratifiedKFold, GridSearchCV
    from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler, OneHotEncoder
    from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer, make_column_selector as selector
    from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
    from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
    from sklearn.neighbors import KNeighborsClassifier
    from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeClassifier
    from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
    from sklearn.naive_bayes import GaussianNB
    from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
    from sklearn.svm import SVC
    from sklearn.metrics import f1_score, classification_report, confusion_matrix, ConfusionMatrixDisplay, accuracy_score
    from collections import namedtuple
    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

    # MLflow PyFunc wrapper for KServe deployment with DataFrame support
    class LabelDecodingWrapper(cf.pyfunc.PythonModel):
        """
        MLflow PyFunc wrapper for KServe with dataframe_split format
        Receives DataFrame, returns class names
        """

        def __init__(self, sklearn_pipeline, class_names, feature_columns):
            self.sklearn_pipeline = sklearn_pipeline
            self.class_names = class_names
            self.feature_columns = feature_columns

        def predict(self, context, model_input):
            import pandas as pd
            import numpy as np

            if not isinstance(model_input, pd.DataFrame):
                raise ValueError(f"Expected DataFrame, got {type(model_input)}")

            # Validate and select features in correct order
            missing_features = set(self.feature_columns) - set(model_input.columns)
            if missing_features:
                raise ValueError(f"Missing features: {missing_features}")

            df = model_input[self.feature_columns]

            # Get numeric predictions and decode to class names
            numeric_preds = self.sklearn_pipeline.predict(df)
            return np.array([self.class_names[int(i)] for i in numeric_preds])

    cf.set_experiment(experiment_id=experiment_id)

    # Load data
    train_df = pd.read_parquet(train_data_path)
    X_train = train_df.drop(columns=['target'])
    y_train = train_df['target'].values

    test_df = pd.read_parquet(test_data_path)
    X_test = test_df.drop(columns=['target'])
    y_test = test_df['target'].values

    # Load config
    with open(preprocessing_config_path, 'r') as f:
        config = json.load(f)

    class_names = config['class_names']
    feature_columns = config['feature_columns']

    print(f"\n Training {model_name}...")
    print(f"   Class names: {class_names}")
    print(f"   Features: {len(feature_columns)}")

    # Preprocessing pipeline
    num_proc = Pipeline(steps=[
        ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="mean")),
        ("scaler", StandardScaler(with_mean=True, with_std=True))
    ])
    cat_proc = Pipeline(steps=[
        ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="most_frequent")),
        ("ohe", OneHotEncoder(handle_unknown="ignore", sparse_output=False))
    ])

    preprocess = ColumnTransformer(
        transformers=[
            ("num", num_proc, selector(dtype_include=np.number)),
            ("cat", cat_proc, selector(dtype_exclude=np.number))
        ],
        remainder="drop"
    )

    # Model selection
    model_map = {
        "KNN": KNeighborsClassifier(),
        "DecisionTree": DecisionTreeClassifier(class_weight="balanced", random_state=42),
        "RandomForest": RandomForestClassifier(class_weight="balanced", random_state=42, n_jobs=-1),
        "NaiveBayes": GaussianNB(),
        "LogisticRegression": LogisticRegression(class_weight="balanced", random_state=42, max_iter=1000),
        "SVM": SVC(class_weight="balanced", random_state=42)
    }

    clf = model_map[model_name]

    # Training pipeline
    training_pipeline = Pipeline([
        ("prep", preprocess), 
        ("clf", clf)
    ])

    with cf.start_run(run_name=f"{model_name}_training") as run:
        run_id = run.info.run_id

        # Log parameters
        cf.log_param("model_type", model_name)
        cf.log_param("n_samples_train", len(X_train))
        cf.log_param("n_samples_test", len(X_test))
        cf.log_param("class_names", ', '.join(class_names))
        cf.log_param("n_classes", len(class_names))

        # GridSearchCV
        cv = StratifiedKFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=42)
        gs = GridSearchCV(
            estimator=training_pipeline,
            param_grid=model_params,
            scoring="f1_macro",
            cv=cv,
            n_jobs=-1,
            refit=True,
            verbose=1
        )

        gs.fit(X_train, y_train)
        best_cv_score = gs.best_score_
        best_params = gs.best_params_

        print(f"-> {model_name}: CV F1 Macro = {best_cv_score:.4f}")
        print(f"   Best params: {best_params}")

        # Log best parameters
        for param_name, param_value in best_params.items():
            cf.log_param(param_name, param_value)

        cf.log_metric("cv_f1_macro", best_cv_score)
        cf.log_metric("cv_std", gs.cv_results_['std_test_score'][gs.best_index_])

        # Log CV results
        cv_results_df = pd.DataFrame(gs.cv_results_)
        cv_results_df.to_csv("cv_results.csv", index=False)
        cf.log_artifact("cv_results.csv")

        best_pipeline = gs.best_estimator_

        # Evaluate on test set
        print(f"\n Evaluating {model_name} on test set...")

        y_pred = best_pipeline.predict(X_test)

        test_f1_macro = f1_score(y_test, y_pred, average='macro')
        test_f1_weighted = f1_score(y_test, y_pred, average='weighted')
        test_accuracy = accuracy_score(y_test, y_pred)

        print(f" Test Performance:")
        print(f"   Accuracy: {test_accuracy:.4f}")
        print(f"   F1 Macro: {test_f1_macro:.4f}")
        print(f"   F1 Weighted: {test_f1_weighted:.4f}")

        cf.log_metric("test_f1_macro", test_f1_macro)
        cf.log_metric("test_f1_weighted", test_f1_weighted)
        cf.log_metric("test_accuracy", test_accuracy)

        # Log per-class metrics
        report = classification_report(y_test, y_pred, target_names=class_names, output_dict=True)
        for label, label_metrics in report.items():
            if isinstance(label_metrics, dict):
                for metric_name, value in label_metrics.items():
                    cf.log_metric(f"test_{label}_{metric_name}", value)

        # Log confusion matrix
        cm = confusion_matrix(y_test, y_pred)
        cm_dict = {"confusion_matrix": cm.tolist(), "labels": class_names}
        with open("confusion_matrix.json", "w") as f:
            json.dump(cm_dict, f, indent=2)
        cf.log_artifact("confusion_matrix.json")

        # Log confusion matrix visualization
        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 8))
        disp = ConfusionMatrixDisplay(confusion_matrix=cm, display_labels=class_names)
        disp.plot(ax=ax, xticks_rotation=45, colorbar=True, cmap='Blues')
        plt.title(f'Confusion Matrix - {model_name}')
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig('confusion_matrix.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
        cf.log_artifact('confusion_matrix.png')
        plt.close()

        # Create PyFunc model wrapper
        wrapped_model = LabelDecodingWrapper(
            sklearn_pipeline=best_pipeline,
            class_names=class_names,
            feature_columns=feature_columns
        )

        # Test with sample data
        sample_input = X_train.head(3)
        sample_pred = wrapped_model.predict(context=None, model_input=sample_input)
        print(f" Sample predictions: {sample_pred}")

        # Log model
        model_info = cf.pyfunc.log_model(
            artifact_path="model",
            python_model=wrapped_model,
            signature=cf.models.infer_signature(sample_input, sample_pred),
            input_example=sample_input
        )

        model_uri = model_info.model_uri

        print(f" Model logged to MLflow:")
        print(f"   Run ID: {run_id}")
        print(f"   Model URI: {model_uri}")

    outputs = namedtuple("Outputs", ["cv_f1_macro", "test_f1_macro", "test_accuracy", "run_id", "model_uri"])
    return outputs(float(best_cv_score), float(test_f1_macro), float(test_accuracy), run_id, model_uri)

def _serialize_float(float_value: float) -> str:
    if isinstance(float_value, str):
        return float_value
    if not isinstance(float_value, (float, int)):
        raise TypeError('Value "{}" has type "{}" instead of float.'.format(
            str(float_value), str(type(float_value))))
    return str(float_value)

def _serialize_str(str_value: str) -> str:
    if not isinstance(str_value, str):
        raise TypeError('Value "{}" has type "{}" instead of str.'.format(
            str(str_value), str(type(str_value))))
    return str_value

import json
import argparse
_parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(prog='Train and evaluate model', description='Train a model with GridSearchCV, evaluate it, and log everything to MLflow')
_parser.add_argument("--train-data", dest="train_data_path", type=str, required=True, default=argparse.SUPPRESS)
_parser.add_argument("--test-data", dest="test_data_path", type=str, required=True, default=argparse.SUPPRESS)
_parser.add_argument("--preprocessing-config", dest="preprocessing_config_path", type=str, required=True, default=argparse.SUPPRESS)
_parser.add_argument("--model-name", dest="model_name", type=str, required=True, default=argparse.SUPPRESS)
_parser.add_argument("--model-params", dest="model_params", type=json.loads, required=True, default=argparse.SUPPRESS)
_parser.add_argument("--experiment-id", dest="experiment_id", type=str, required=True, default=argparse.SUPPRESS)
_parser.add_argument("----output-paths", dest="_output_paths", type=str, nargs=5)
_parsed_args = vars(_parser.parse_args())
_output_files = _parsed_args.pop("_output_paths", [])

_outputs = train_and_evaluate_model(**_parsed_args)

_output_serializers = [
    _serialize_float,
    _serialize_float,
    _serialize_float,
    _serialize_str,
    _serialize_str,

]

import os
for idx, output_file in enumerate(_output_files):
    try:
        os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(output_file))
    except OSError:
        pass
    with open(output_file, 'w') as f:
        f.write(_output_serializers[idx](_outputs[idx]))

        cf.log_param("n_samples_test", len(X_test))
        cf.log_param("class_names", ', '.join(class_names))
        cf.log_param("n_classes", len(class_names))

        # GridSearchCV
        cv = StratifiedKFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=42)
        gs = GridSearchCV(
            estimator=training_pipeline,
            param_grid=model_params,
            scoring="f1_macro",
            cv=cv,
            n_jobs=-1,
            refit=True,
            verbose=1
        )

        gs.fit(X_train, y_train)
        best_cv_score = gs.best_score_
        best_params = gs.best_params_

        print(f"-> {model_name}: CV F1 Macro = {best_cv_score:.4f}")
        print(f"   Best params: {best_params}")

        # Log best parameters
        for param_name, param_value in best_params.items():
            cf.log_param(param_name, param_value)

        cf.log_metric("cv_f1_macro", best_cv_score)
        cf.log_metric("cv_std", gs.cv_results_['std_test_score'][gs.best_index_])

        # Log CV results
        cv_results_df = pd.DataFrame(gs.cv_results_)
        cv_results_df.to_csv("cv_results.csv", index=False)
        cf.log_artifact("cv_results.csv")

        best_pipeline = gs.best_estimator_

        # Evaluate on test set
        print(f"\n Evaluating {model_name} on test set...")

        y_pred = best_pipeline.predict(X_test)

        test_f1_macro = f1_score(y_test, y_pred, average='macro')
        test_f1_weighted = f1_score(y_test, y_pred, average='weighted')
        test_accuracy = accuracy_score(y_test, y_pred)

        print(f" Test Performance:")
        print(f"   Accuracy: {test_accuracy:.4f}")
        print(f"   F1 Macro: {test_f1_macro:.4f}")
        print(f"   F1 Weighted: {test_f1_weighted:.4f}")

        cf.log_metric("test_f1_macro", test_f1_macro)
        cf.log_metric("test_f1_weighted", test_f1_weighted)
        cf.log_metric("test_accuracy", test_accuracy)

        # Log per-class metrics
        report = classification_report(y_test, y_pred, target_names=class_names, output_dict=True)
        for label, label_metrics in report.items():
            if isinstance(label_metrics, dict):
                for metric_name, value in label_metrics.items():
                    cf.log_metric(f"test_{label}_{metric_name}", value)

        # Log confusion matrix
        cm = confusion_matrix(y_test, y_pred)
        cm_dict = {"confusion_matrix": cm.tolist(), "labels": class_names}
        with open("confusion_matrix.json", "w") as f:
            json.dump(cm_dict, f, indent=2)
        cf.log_artifact("confusion_matrix.json")

        # Log confusion matrix visualization
        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 8))
        disp = ConfusionMatrixDisplay(confusion_matrix=cm, display_labels=class_names)
        disp.plot(ax=ax, xticks_rotation=45, colorbar=True, cmap='Blues')
        plt.title(f'Confusion Matrix - {model_name}')
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig('confusion_matrix.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
        cf.log_artifact('confusion_matrix.png')
        plt.close()

        # Create PyFunc model wrapper
        wrapped_model = LabelDecodingWrapper(
            sklearn_pipeline=best_pipeline,
            class_names=class_names,
            feature_columns=feature_columns
        )

        # Test with sample data
        sample_input = X_train.head(3)
        sample_pred = wrapped_model.predict(context=None, model_input=sample_input)
        print(f" Sample predictions: {sample_pred}")

        # Log model
        model_info = cf.pyfunc.log_model(
            artifact_path="model",
            python_model=wrapped_model,
            signature=cf.models.infer_signature(sample_input, sample_pred),
            input_example=sample_input
        )

        model_uri = model_info.model_uri

        print(f" Model logged to MLflow:")
        print(f"   Run ID: {run_id}")
        print(f"   Model URI: {model_uri}")

    outputs = namedtuple("Outputs", ["cv_f1_macro", "test_f1_macro", "test_accuracy", "run_id", "model_uri"])
    return outputs(float(best_cv_score), float(test_f1_macro), float(test_accuracy), run_id, model_uri)

def _serialize_float(float_value: float) -> str:
    if isinstance(float_value, str):
        return float_value
    if not isinstance(float_value, (float, int)):
        raise TypeError('Value "{}" has type "{}" instead of float.'.format(
            str(float_value), str(type(float_value))))
    return str(float_value)

def _serialize_str(str_value: str) -> str:
    if not isinstance(str_value, str):
        raise TypeError('Value "{}" has type "{}" instead of str.'.format(
            str(str_value), str(type(str_value))))
    return str_value

def train_and_evaluate_model(
    train_data_path,
    test_data_path,
    preprocessing_config_path,
    model_name,
    model_params,
    experiment_id
):
    """Train a model with GridSearchCV, evaluate it, and log everything to MLflow"""
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import json
    import mlflow as cf
    import mlflow.pyfunc
    from sklearn.model_selection import StratifiedKFold, GridSearchCV
    from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler, OneHotEncoder
    from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer, make_column_selector as selector
    from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
    from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
    from sklearn.neighbors import KNeighborsClassifier
    from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeClassifier
    from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
    from sklearn.naive_bayes import GaussianNB
    from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
    from sklearn.svm import SVC
    from sklearn.metrics import f1_score, classification_report, confusion_matrix, ConfusionMatrixDisplay, accuracy_score
    from collections import namedtuple
    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

    # MLflow PyFunc wrapper for KServe deployment with DataFrame support
    class LabelDecodingWrapper(cf.pyfunc.PythonModel):
        """
        MLflow PyFunc wrapper for KServe with dataframe_split format
        Receives DataFrame, returns class names
        """

        def __init__(self, sklearn_pipeline, class_names, feature_columns):
            self.sklearn_pipeline = sklearn_pipeline
            self.class_names = class_names
            self.feature_columns = feature_columns

        def predict(self, context, model_input):
            import pandas as pd
            import numpy as np

            if not isinstance(model_input, pd.DataFrame):
                raise ValueError(f"Expected DataFrame, got {type(model_input)}")

            # Validate and select features in correct order
            missing_features = set(self.feature_columns) - set(model_input.columns)
            if missing_features:
                raise ValueError(f"Missing features: {missing_features}")

            df = model_input[self.feature_columns]

            # Get numeric predictions and decode to class names
            numeric_preds = self.sklearn_pipeline.predict(df)
            return np.array([self.class_names[int(i)] for i in numeric_preds])

    cf.set_experiment(experiment_id=experiment_id)

    # Load data
    train_df = pd.read_parquet(train_data_path)
    X_train = train_df.drop(columns=['target'])
    y_train = train_df['target'].values

    test_df = pd.read_parquet(test_data_path)
    X_test = test_df.drop(columns=['target'])
    y_test = test_df['target'].values

    # Load config
    with open(preprocessing_config_path, 'r') as f:
        config = json.load(f)

    class_names = config['class_names']
    feature_columns = config['feature_columns']

    print(f"\n Training {model_name}...")
    print(f"   Class names: {class_names}")
    print(f"   Features: {len(feature_columns)}")

    # Preprocessing pipeline
    num_proc = Pipeline(steps=[
        ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="mean")),
        ("scaler", StandardScaler(with_mean=True, with_std=True))
    ])
    cat_proc = Pipeline(steps=[
        ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="most_frequent")),
        ("ohe", OneHotEncoder(handle_unknown="ignore", sparse_output=False))
    ])

    preprocess = ColumnTransformer(
        transformers=[
            ("num", num_proc, selector(dtype_include=np.number)),
            ("cat", cat_proc, selector(dtype_exclude=np.number))
        ],
        remainder="drop"
    )

    # Model selection
    model_map = {
        "KNN": KNeighborsClassifier(),
        "DecisionTree": DecisionTreeClassifier(class_weight="balanced", random_state=42),
        "RandomForest": RandomForestClassifier(class_weight="balanced", random_state=42, n_jobs=-1),
        "NaiveBayes": GaussianNB(),
        "LogisticRegression": LogisticRegression(class_weight="balanced", random_state=42, max_iter=1000),
        "SVM": SVC(class_weight="balanced", random_state=42)
    }

    clf = model_map[model_name]

    # Training pipeline
    training_pipeline = Pipeline([
        ("prep", preprocess), 
        ("clf", clf)
    ])

    with cf.start_run(run_name=f"{model_name}_training") as run:
        run_id = run.info.run_id

        # Log parameters
        cf.log_param("model_type", model_name)
        cf.log_param("n_samples_train", len(X_train))
        cf.log_param("n_samples_test", len(X_test))
        cf.log_param("class_names", ', '.join(class_names))
        cf.log_param("n_classes", len(class_names))

        # GridSearchCV
        cv = StratifiedKFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=42)
        gs = GridSearchCV(
            estimator=training_pipeline,
            param_grid=model_params,
            scoring="f1_macro",
            cv=cv,
            n_jobs=-1,
            refit=True,
            verbose=1
        )

        gs.fit(X_train, y_train)
        best_cv_score = gs.best_score_
        best_params = gs.best_params_

        print(f"-> {model_name}: CV F1 Macro = {best_cv_score:.4f}")
        print(f"   Best params: {best_params}")

        # Log best parameters
        for param_name, param_value in best_params.items():
            cf.log_param(param_name, param_value)

        cf.log_metric("cv_f1_macro", best_cv_score)
        cf.log_metric("cv_std", gs.cv_results_['std_test_score'][gs.best_index_])

        # Log CV results
        cv_results_df = pd.DataFrame(gs.cv_results_)
        cv_results_df.to_csv("cv_results.csv", index=False)
        cf.log_artifact("cv_results.csv")

        best_pipeline = gs.best_estimator_

        # Evaluate on test set
        print(f"\n Evaluating {model_name} on test set...")

        y_pred = best_pipeline.predict(X_test)

        test_f1_macro = f1_score(y_test, y_pred, average='macro')
        test_f1_weighted = f1_score(y_test, y_pred, average='weighted')
        test_accuracy = accuracy_score(y_test, y_pred)

        print(f" Test Performance:")
        print(f"   Accuracy: {test_accuracy:.4f}")
        print(f"   F1 Macro: {test_f1_macro:.4f}")
        print(f"   F1 Weighted: {test_f1_weighted:.4f}")

        cf.log_metric("test_f1_macro", test_f1_macro)
        cf.log_metric("test_f1_weighted", test_f1_weighted)
        cf.log_metric("test_accuracy", test_accuracy)

        # Log per-class metrics
        report = classification_report(y_test, y_pred, target_names=class_names, output_dict=True)
        for label, label_metrics in report.items():
            if isinstance(label_metrics, dict):
                for metric_name, value in label_metrics.items():
                    cf.log_metric(f"test_{label}_{metric_name}", value)

        # Log confusion matrix
        cm = confusion_matrix(y_test, y_pred)
        cm_dict = {"confusion_matrix": cm.tolist(), "labels": class_names}
        with open("confusion_matrix.json", "w") as f:
            json.dump(cm_dict, f, indent=2)
        cf.log_artifact("confusion_matrix.json")

        # Log confusion matrix visualization
        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 8))
        disp = ConfusionMatrixDisplay(confusion_matrix=cm, display_labels=class_names)
        disp.plot(ax=ax, xticks_rotation=45, colorbar=True, cmap='Blues')
        plt.title(f'Confusion Matrix - {model_name}')
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig('confusion_matrix.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
        cf.log_artifact('confusion_matrix.png')
        plt.close()

        # Create PyFunc model wrapper
        wrapped_model = LabelDecodingWrapper(
            sklearn_pipeline=best_pipeline,
            class_names=class_names,
            feature_columns=feature_columns
        )

        # Test with sample data
        sample_input = X_train.head(3)
        sample_pred = wrapped_model.predict(context=None, model_input=sample_input)
        print(f" Sample predictions: {sample_pred}")

        # Log model
        model_info = cf.pyfunc.log_model(
            artifact_path="model",
            python_model=wrapped_model,
            signature=cf.models.infer_signature(sample_input, sample_pred),
            input_example=sample_input
        )

        model_uri = model_info.model_uri

        print(f" Model logged to MLflow:")
        print(f"   Run ID: {run_id}")
        print(f"   Model URI: {model_uri}")

    outputs = namedtuple("Outputs", ["cv_f1_macro", "test_f1_macro", "test_accuracy", "run_id", "model_uri"])
    return outputs(float(best_cv_score), float(test_f1_macro), float(test_accuracy), run_id, model_uri)

def _serialize_float(float_value: float) -> str:
    if isinstance(float_value, str):
        return float_value
    if not isinstance(float_value, (float, int)):
        raise TypeError('Value "{}" has type "{}" instead of float.'.format(
            str(float_value), str(type(float_value))))
    return str(float_value)

def _serialize_str(str_value: str) -> str:
    if not isinstance(str_value, str):

    return str_value

        cm_dict = {"confusion_matrix": cm.tolist(), "labels": class_names}
        with open("confusion_matrix.json", "w") as f:
            json.dump(cm_dict, f, indent=2)
        cf.log_artifact("confusion_matrix.json")

        # Log confusion matrix visualization
        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 8))
        disp = ConfusionMatrixDisplay(confusion_matrix=cm, display_labels=class_names)
        disp.plot(ax=ax, xticks_rotation=45, colorbar=True, cmap='Blues')
        plt.title(f'Confusion Matrix - {model_name}')
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig('confusion_matrix.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
        cf.log_artifact('confusion_matrix.png')
        plt.close()

        # Create PyFunc model wrapper
        wrapped_model = LabelDecodingWrapper(
            sklearn_pipeline=best_pipeline,
            class_names=class_names,
            feature_columns=feature_columns
        )

        # Test with sample data
        sample_input = X_train.head(3)
        sample_pred = wrapped_model.predict(context=None, model_input=sample_input)
        print(f" Sample predictions: {sample_pred}")

        # Log model
        model_info = cf.pyfunc.log_model(
            artifact_path="model",
            python_model=wrapped_model,
            signature=cf.models.infer_signature(sample_input, sample_pred),
            input_example=sample_input
        )

        model_uri = model_info.model_uri

        print(f" Model logged to MLflow:")
        print(f"   Run ID: {run_id}")
        print(f"   Model URI: {model_uri}")

    outputs = namedtuple("Outputs", ["cv_f1_macro", "test_f1_macro", "test_accuracy", "run_id", "model_uri"])
    return outputs(float(best_cv_score), float(test_f1_macro), float(test_accuracy), run_id, model_uri)
